articles.title: Más aire para el dólar: volvió a caer la demanda para atesoramiento y quedan u$s3.200 millones para liquidar de ONs
articles.content: Así lo mostró el vicepresidente del BCRA, Vladimir Werning, en una exposición frente a inversores en EEUU.

articles.title: El sistema previsional y su desequilibrio: hay solo dos aportantes formales por jubilado
articles.content: La caída del empleo formal y el avance del monotributo deterioran el financiamiento del sistema, que depende cada vez más de transferencias estatales y pierde poder adquisitivo.

articles.title: ATE realizará un paro nacional por reclamo salarial y no descarta bloquear los accesos a aeropuertos
articles.content: El gremio de estatales se concentrará este martes en diferentes puntos del país, con epicentro en las inmediaciones del Aeroparque Jorge Newbery. Aseguran que los trabajadores públicos perdieron más de $11 millones en dos años de gobierno de Javier Milei.

articles.title: Ahorro en dólar: cómo viajar al exterior sin pagar un peso en alojamiento
articles.content: Esta alternativa no es muy conocida, pero te permite recorrer distintos destinos del mundo con una experiencia distinta y muy atractiva.

articles.title: Un informe de J.P. Morgan advierte que creció fuerte la morosidad en los préstamos de Mercado Pago
articles.content: Un informe del JP Morgan reveló detalles sobre la situación de la cartera total de crédito de Mercado Libre.

articles.title: Cuánto debés invertir en un plazo fijo de 30 días para ganar $30.000
articles.content: El rendimiento varía según la tasa del banco y si la operación se realiza por sucursal o de forma electrónica: conocé los detalles.

articles.title: Cuánto sale un iPhone reacondicionado en 2026: ¿vale la pena?
articles.content: Muchas personas quieren acceder a la compra de uno de estos dispositivos, pero por su precio buscan otras alternativas.

articles.title: El superávit comercial creció a u$s2.523 millones en marzo, tercer máximo de la era Milei
articles.content: Las exportaciones pegaron un salto mensual de casi 20%, el más elevado desde enero de 2021. Las importaciones se mantuvieron estancadas.

articles.title: Uno de los economistas más escuchados por el Gobierno advirtió por el desempleo en el conurbano
articles.content: El economista Ricardo Arriazu advirtió que la dinámica actual destruye más puestos de trabajo de los que genera y alertó sobre el impacto político en el Gran Buenos Aires. También pidió bajar la tasa de interés y avanzar con medidas de contención.

articles.title: La industria metalúrgica cayó 4,1% en marzo y la capacidad instalada tocó su nivel más bajo en cuatro años
articles.content: El sector acumula una contracción del 6,9% en el primer trimestre de 2026. ADIMRA advirtió por la falta de demanda interna, caída del empleo y márgenes cada vez más ajustados.

articles.title: Christine Lagarde: The energy shock: where we stand and what we need to know
Victory Giant Surges in Debut After Biggest Hong Kong Listing This Year
articles.content: Victory Giant Technology Huizhou Co., a Chinese supplier of Nvidia Corp., surged as much as 60% in its Hong Kong trading debut after raising $2.6 billion in the city’s largest listing in seven months.

articles.title: Hedge Funds Beef Up Bearish Dollar Bets as Haven Demand Sinks
articles.content: Hedge funds and other asset managers are ramping up bets on dollar weakness using options, in a sign the market is turning even further against the US currency as haven demand wanes.

articles.title: Chinese Copper Output Hits Record on Tailwind From Sulfuric Acid
articles.content: Copper smelters in China churned out a record volume of refined metal last month, as surging prices of byproduct sulfuric acid encouraged higher output and aided the industry’s profitability.

articles.title: Indian Stocks Face Fresh Hurdle as Fuel Price-Hike Risk Looms
articles.content: If pump prices do rise, the effects will be felt across the broader economy.

articles.title: JPMorgan Aims for China Approval of Active ETF Launch This Year
articles.content: JPMorgan Chase & Co. is working toward getting approval from Chinese securities regulators to launch actively managed exchange-traded funds in the country for the first time.

articles.title: Australian Gasoline Prices Fall for Third Straight Week on Government Interventions
articles.content: Australian gasoline prices fell for a third straight week, as government measures eased some of the pain at the pump triggered by the war in Iran.

articles.title: MTR Seeks $1.9 Billion in Public Hong Kong Dollar Bond Sale
articles.content: MTR Corp Ltd. began marketing its first-ever Hong Kong dollar public bond offering Tuesday, adding to an uptick of borrowing in the local currency debt market.

articles.title: MSCI Delays High-Stakes Indonesia Review After Downgrade Fears Spooked Investors
articles.content: Indonesian stocks slid after MSCI Inc. extended the review period for the nation’s stock market status to June as it assesses the impact of recent regulatory reforms.

articles.title: Inflation Not Expected to Have Big Impact on Global Economy: Capital Group
articles.content: Capital Group's Noriko Chen says this market disruption looks "a little bit more temporary" as it's mainly about short-term energy supplies rather than oil and gas production. She tells Bloomberg Television that while inflation will run higher, it's not expected to have a big impact on the global economy. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Asian Stocks Gain, Oil Dips on Hopes of Iran Talks: Markets Wrap
articles.content: Asian stocks rose as signs Iran may join talks with the US fostered cautious optimism about progress in the Middle East ahead of a looming ceasefire deadline. Oil dropped.

articles.title: China’s Wanhua Bets on Overseas Growth as Trade Risks Mount
articles.content: China’s Wanhua Chemical Group Co. pledged to accelerate its overseas expansion to help counter rising trade risks after the petrochemical giant reported an increase in first-quarter earnings.

articles.title: South Korean Battery Stocks Rally on Mercedes-Benz Partnership
articles.content: Shares of South Korea’s top battery makers jumped Tuesday after clinching deals with Mercedes-Benz Group AG, providing a welcome boost to a sector that has been hit by the slowing global transition to electric vehicles.

articles.title: AI Boom Propels Kospi to Record as Tech Giants Defy War Jitters
articles.content: Investor optimism that the worst of the Iran war may be over was on full display Tuesday, when the return of the AI trade drove South Korean stocks to a record and spurred another rally in Taiwan.

articles.title: Global Stock Rally Resumes, Oil Dips on Iran Talks: Markets Wrap
articles.content: A rally in global equities resumed after a brief pause, as signs Iran may join talks with the US helped add to optimism over progress in the Middle East ahead of a looming ceasefire deadline. Oil declined.

articles.title: Maga Inc builds nearly $350mn war chest ahead of midterm elections
articles.content: Pro-Trump Super Pac raises $35mn in March with donations from billionaire Diane Hendricks and Andreessen Horowitz

articles.title: Japan lifts ban on lethal arms exports
articles.content: Major shift from pacifist stance will allow defence contractors to target international markets

articles.title: Fitch Cuts Philippines’ Rating Outlook as Growth Prospects Dim
articles.content: Fitch Ratings revised its outlook on the Philippines’ credit rating to negative from stable, saying a decline in public investment and rising energy costs have created risks to the Southeast Asian nation’s economic growth.

articles.title: Gold Falls as Traders Weigh Next Round of US-Iran Peace Talks
articles.content: Gold declined as traders weighed prospects for a negotiated settlement to the war in Iran that’s disrupted global energy supplies and heightened inflation risks.

articles.title: Chinese Manufacturing Growth Overtaken by Finance Amid IPO Boom
articles.content: Chinese manufacturing grew slower than finance for the first time in years, turbocharged by capital raised through share sales during a quarter that saw a surprise rebound in the economy.

articles.title: Hong Kong Bourse Plans to Start Zero-Day Options in Early 2027
articles.content: Hong Kong’s stock exchange is working on introducing options expiring within a day next year, joining a global trend toward shorter-dated bets that’s sent US derivatives volume ballooning.

articles.title: CATL Share Sale’s Tightening Discount Shows China Tech Momentum
articles.content: Contemporary Amperex Technology Co.’s share sale at a 5.1% discount, with demand running more than twice the available supply, underscores investor appetite for Chinese technology stocks even after the battery maker’s 20% rally this year.

articles.title: It Doesn’t Matter What Kevin Warsh Has to Say
articles.content: The Powell case and future economic challenges are out of the Fed nominee’s hands.

articles.title: EQT Raises $15.6B for Asia Private Equity Fund
articles.content:
Jean Eric Salata, Chairman at EQT Asia, discusses how the firm raised $15.6 billion for its latest private equity Asia fund, as global investors look beyond the US amid elevated uncertainty. He speaks with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts on Bloomberg: The Asia Trade. 

 (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Mercuria Seeks New Liquidity in Asia With $200 Million Financing
articles.content: Mercuria Energy Group Ltd. plans to raise at least $200 million of financing in Asia, according to people familiar with the matter, as commodity traders seek alternative sources of funding at a time when the Iran war has raised the cost of buying cargoes.

articles.title: ‘Kiss of death’: How the US killed a Swiss merchant bank
articles.content: MBaer survived years of scrutiny at home — before Washington stepped in to seal its fate

articles.content: The outgoing North Carolina senator is refusing to back down in fight over president’s probe into Jerome Powell

articles.content: Marion Maréchal returns to family fold and will back Rassemblement National’s presidential campaign

articles.title: Wall Street Banks Flag Risks for Kenya’s Shilling as War Rages On
articles.content: Kenya’s shilling is among the most vulnerable currencies in Africa, according to strategists at major banks, who predict further weakness as elevated oil prices pile pressure on the economy.

articles.title: FTSE 100 Poised to Rise as Iran Optimism Returns
Pakistan’s Bid to Mediate US‑Iran Talks Amid Uncertainty
articles.content:
Pakistan is positioning itself as a potential mediator between the United States and Iran, with officials preparing to host a possible second round of talks despite lingering uncertainty. The effort highlights Islamabad’s diplomatic outreach at a time of heightened regional tensions and fragile US‑Iran engagement.
The discussions matter as any renewed dialogue could ease pressure in the Middle East and shape regional security calculations for South Asia and beyond.
Sherry Rehman, former Pakistani ambassador to the US, spoke with Paul Allen on Insight with Haslinda Amin to discuss Pakistan’s role and the challenges ahead. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: What to Expect From Apple's Incoming CEO John Ternus | The China Show 4/21/2026
articles.content: “Bloomberg: The China Show” is your definitive source for news and analysis on the world's second-biggest economy. From politics and policy to tech and trends, Yvonne Man and Avril Hong give global investors unique insight, delivering in-depth discussions with the newsmakers who matter. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: RBI Flags Inflation Spillover Risks From Middle East Conflict
articles.content: India’s central bank governor said the Middle East crisis could fuel persistent inflation if supply disruptions endure, given the country’s deep economic links to the region.

articles.title: Data-Center Cooling Stocks Sink in China on Competition Concerns
articles.content: Shares of Chinese liquid-cooling providers fell after a closely watched company reported earnings that missed expectations, sparking concerns over intensifying competition.

articles.title: Indian Debt Funds Cut Hedges as Oil Risks Inflate Rate-Hike Bets
articles.content: Some Indian debt fund managers are cutting back on interest-rate hedges against their bond holdings, saying markets have already priced in an excessive rise in borrowing costs driven by a surge in oil prices.

articles.title: MSCI boots Indonesian tycoon-owned stocks from indices
articles.content: Index provider delays decision on whether to downgrade south-east Asia’s largest economy to frontier market

articles.title: UK Job Cuts Suggest Companies Turning Cautious on Iran War Shock
articles.content: UK businesses stepped up job cutting in March, a sign that the Iran war is causing fresh caution in the labor market after warnings Britain will be one of the hardest hit advanced economies from the energy shock.

articles.title: Mythos Access Must Be Granted on Level Playing Field, Nagel Says
articles.content: Anthropic’s Mythos model should be shared with affected organizations to ensure a level playing field in assessing its uses and dangers, according to Bundesbank President Joachim Nagel.

articles.title: Indonesia Stocks Slide After MSCI Delays High-Stakes Review
articles.content: Indonesian equities slid after MSCI Inc. extended its market status review to June to assess regulatory reforms and warned it would remove major stocks with tightly-held ownership from its indexes.

articles.title: China Bonds Set for Best Month Since October on Ample Cash
articles.content: China’s benchmark bonds are heading for their best month since October, as abundant liquidity offsets concerns over upcoming debt supply.

articles.title: Brevan Howard Plans Tokyo Office, to Hire Traders in Hub Boost
articles.content: Brevan Howard Asset Management plans to open a Tokyo office this summer, joining the rush of global finance giants seeking to capitalize on trading opportunities in the Japanese market, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

articles.title: AB Foods to Separate Its Budget Fashion Chain Primark
articles.content: Associated British Foods Plc will separate budget apparel chain Primark from the rest of its business, in a breakup of one of the UK’s biggest conglomerates.

articles.title: Poland Fines Luxury Car Trader for Illegal Exports to Russia
articles.content: Poland has fined a local company 20 million zloty ($5.5 million) for intentionally violating European Union sanctions by selling luxury cars to Russia, the country’s tax authority said.

articles.title: India Bond Clearing House Said to Apply for European Recognition
articles.content: India’s sovereign debt clearinghouse sent an application to European authorities to be recognized as a permitted trading counterparty, according to people familiar with the developments, potentially paving the way for lenders like Deutsche Bank AG and BNP Paribas SA to trade bonds more easily in the country.

articles.title: JPMorgan Says Investors Are Focused on Corporate Resilience
articles.content: JPMorgan Private Bank Global Investment Strategist Madison Faller says the corporate earnings season showed that the "fundamentals remain intact" in spite of the uncertainties around the war in the Middle East. She speaks on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Ex-Swedbank CEO Bonnesen Fully Cleared in Dirty Money Case
articles.content: Swedbank AB’s former chief executive officer, Birgitte Bonnesen, was acquitted by Sweden’s Supreme Court in a case related to one of Europe’s largest money laundering scandals.

articles.title: Commerzbank Rejects UniCredit’s ‘Hostile’ Approach
articles.content: German lender Commerzbank AG says it has formally rejected Italian rival UniCredit Spa’s latest takeover proposal, saying it lacks value and undermines trust. Bloomberg's Tom Metcalf reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: France Sees Up to €6 Billion Budget Impact From Iran War
articles.content: The war in the Middle East will have an estimated impact on the French budget of as much as €6 billion ($7.1 billion), Finance Minister Roland Lescure said in an interview with RTL radio.

articles.title: US Stocks Edge Up as Traders Await Iran Update: Markets Wrap
articles.content: Stocks rose modestly as traders grew more encouraged that Iran will join talks that could extend a Middle East truce and restore oil flows. Treasuries and the dollar wavered ahead of Kevin Warsh’s Senate hearing.

articles.title: Bawag Plans Dividend Cut, SRTs to Help Fund Permanent TSB Deal
articles.content: Bawag Group AG will reduce investor payouts and rely on significant risk transfers to fund the €1.62 billion ($1.9 billion) acquisition of Ireland’s third-largest lender, Permanent TSB.

articles.title: Swiss Watch Exports Dipped in March Amid Iran War and High Costs
articles.content: Swiss watch exports slid in March as the industry faced disruptions in the Middle East and contended with high precious metal costs.

articles.title: AI Optimism Pushing Stocks Higher Despite Iran Risks: 3-Minutes MLIV
articles.content: Anna Edwards, Guy Johnson, Tom Mackenzie and Adam Linton break down today's key themes for analysts and investors on "Bloomberg: The Opening Trade." (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Apple chief Tim Cook to hand over to John Ternus in September
articles.content: Head of hardware to become next chief executive while Cook will become chair of the iPhone maker

articles.title: Euro Attracts Bullish Options Buyers as Currency Goes Off Script
articles.content: The euro is the second-best G-10 performer against the dollar over the past month, defying expectations that the energy shock caused by the Middle East war would hit Europe’s economy and drag the currency lower.

articles.title: Guindos Says ECB Needs to Be Prudent, Keep a Cool Head on Rates
articles.content: Uncertainty over the Iran war means the European Central Bank should take a measured approach to setting interest rates, according to Vice President Luis de Guindos.

articles.title: Bawag Plans Dividend Limit, SRTs to Help Fund Irish Deal
articles.content: Bawag Group AG will pause investor payouts and rely on significant risk transfers to fund the €1.62 billion ($1.9 billion) acquisition of Ireland’s third-largest lender, Permanent TSB.

articles.title: Indian Wind Turbine Giant Says Nation Set to Meet 2030 Target
articles.content: Suzlon Energy Ltd., India’s largest wind turbine maker, said the nation is on course to hit its goal of 100 gigawatts of wind capacity by the end of the decade, as installations accelerate to meet growing demand for round-the-clock supply.

articles.title: US Stock Futures Edge Up as Traders Focus on Iran: Markets Wrap
articles.content: Stocks rose modestly as traders waited to see if Iran will join talks that could extend a truce in the Middle East and help restore oil flows. The dollar advanced and Treasuries wavered ahead of Kevin Warsh’s Senate hearing.

articles.title: Oil Prices Don’t Reflect Scale of Supply Hit, Analysts Say
articles.content: Oil prices don’t fully reflect the largest supply disruption ever, after the Iran war effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz, some of the market’s top analysts said.

articles.title: UN Agency Prepares Hormuz Evacuation Plan for Hundreds of Ships
articles.content: The International Maritime Organization is working on an evacuation plan for hundreds of ships that have been stuck in the Persian Gulf since US and Israeli strikes on Iran began more than seven weeks ago, according to Secretary General Arsenio Dominguez.

articles.title: BOJ Flags Risk to Japan Bonds From Foreign Hedge Funds Unwinding
articles.content: The Bank of Japan cautioned that a potential unwinding of positions by hedge funds globally may spread to Japan’s bond market.

articles.title: Middle East Conflict Clouds Thai Banks Outlook as Profits Slip
articles.content: Two major Thai banks struck a cautious tone on their 2026 outlook after reporting weak first-quarter net income, underscoring growing risks from Middle East conflict and a global energy shock.

articles.title: Carlsberg Dumps Coca-Cola, Bets on Pepsi in Nordic Markets
articles.content: Carlsberg A/S is preparing a major shift in its soft drinks strategy, replacing Coke with Pepsi across much of Northern Europe in a move set to reshape the region’s beverage market.

articles.title: US Stock Futures Rise as Traders Await Iran Update: Markets Wrap
articles.content: Stocks rose as traders waited to see if Iran will join talks that could advance a Middle East peace deal and help restore oil flows. The dollar gained and Treasuries wavered ahead of Kevin Warsh’s Senate hearing.

articles.title: Narrow US Stocks Rally Shows Investors Are Cautious
articles.content: Low volume, narrow leadership show some traders are unconvinced

articles.title: Gunvor Says Huge Buyout Loan From Ex-CEO No Growth Barrier
articles.content: A multibillion-dollar loan from former owner Torbjörn Törnqvist won’t constrain energy trader Gunvor Group’s growth, its new chief executive officer said.

articles.title: CIC, NBIM, BlackRock Said to Invest in Victory Giant Listing
articles.content: Victory Giant Technology Huizhou Co. has attracted Chinese and global investors including China Investment Corp. and Norges Bank Investment Management, the world’s largest sovereign fund, to its $2.6 billion share sale, according to people familiar with the matter.

articles.title: Pakistan Receives $1 Billion Saudi Boost As UAE Seeks Repayment
articles.content: Pakistan has received the final $1 billion tranche of a $3 billion support package from Saudi Arabia, shoring up the South Asian nation’s foreign exchange reserves after the UAE declined to roll over its debt.

articles.title: Emerging-Market Assets Eye Record Close as Iran Talks Loom
articles.content: Emerging-market stocks climbed toward a record high, lifted by technology shares, as investors weighed whether Iran will join talks that could extend a Middle East truce and help restore energy flows.

articles.title: Oil Slips on Signs Iran Will Attend Talks With US in Pakistan
articles.content: Oil dipped on signs that Iran will attend talks with the US to end a war that has upended energy markets, as a ceasefire is set to end.

articles.title: India’s Rupee Slips as RBI Lets Banks Resume Offshore Contracts
articles.content: The rupee ended lower after the central bank rolled back some restrictions it had imposed on currency market transactions, including allowing banks to offer offshore derivatives linked to the currency.

articles.title: Qualcomm’s Best Run in Years Barely Puts a Dent in Brutal Selloff
articles.content: By most objective measures, Qualcomm Inc. appears to be in the midst of a stock market heater.

articles.title: Stocks Get Earnings Boost as Traders Focus on Iran: Markets Wrap
articles.content: Stocks rose amid strong corporate earnings as traders waited to see if Iran will join talks that could restore oil flows from the Middle East. The dollar gained and Treasuries wavered ahead of Kevin Warsh’s Senate hearing.

articles.title: Russia’s Grip on Bulgaria Will Test Ambition to Tackle Graft
articles.content: Bulgaria’s political and economic ties to Russia pose the biggest risk to Prime Minister-elect Rumen Radev’s plan to fight corruption.

articles.title: Russian Steel Use Drops 15% in First Quarter as Economy Slows
articles.content: A decline in Russian steel consumption accelerated in the first quarter as the economy slowed, according to Severstal PJSC, one of the country’s top steelmakers.

articles.title: Hedge Funds Beef Up Bearish Dollar Bets as Haven Demand Ebbs
articles.content: Hedge funds and other asset managers are ramping up bets on dollar weakness using options, in a sign the market is turning even further against the US currency as haven demand wanes.

articles.title: Jefferies Picks Tanks and Ammo Over Air Defense After War Slump
articles.content: Shares in European armored vehicle and ammunition makers look attractive again after falling sharply since the start of the Iran war as investors prioritized air defense, according to analysts at Jefferies.

articles.title: Is Ternus the leader Apple needs for the AI era?
articles.content: Tim Cook’s replacement must lead iPhone-maker through industry shift

articles.title: Nicolás Dujovne estimó una inflación de hasta 29% en 2026 y advirtió que bajarla "no es un proceso lineal"
articles.content: El exministro de Economía sostuvo que la desaceleración será más lenta de lo previsto por el Gobierno. Señaló que la inflación enfrenta avances y retrocesos y remarcó la importancia de la prudencia fiscal.

articles.title: ATE realiza un paro nacional por reclamo salarial y crece la tensión en los aeropuertos
articles.content: El gremio de estatales se concentrará este martes en diferentes puntos del país, con epicentro en las inmediaciones del Aeroparque Jorge Newbery. Aseguran que los trabajadores públicos perdieron más de $11 millones en dos años de gobierno de Javier Milei.

articles.title: Desequilibrios externos: qué dice el FMI sobre las políticas industriales, los aranceles y el ajuste macro
articles.content: El organismo financiero internacional enriqueció el debate sobre los desequilibrios de la Cuenta corriente con un análisis global sobre las políticas industriales y arancelarias, concluyendo que las políticas macroeconómicas tradicionales siguen siendo los principales impulsores de los saldos externos y constituyen la mejor herramienta para corregir los desequilibrios de la cuenta corriente.

articles.title: Inflación: prevén que en abril se cortará la racha alcista de 10 meses y el IPC perforará el 3%
articles.content: Según distintos relevamientos privados, el cuarto mes del año aparece como un punto de inflexión. Sin embargo, el proceso de desinflación todavía enfrenta obstáculos.

articles.title: S&P 500 Futures Rally as Traders Await Updates on Iran, Warsh
articles.content: US stock-index futures rose before the bell on Tuesday as traders awaited developments on Iran and Kevin Warsh’s Senate hearing amid the latest round of corporate earnings.

articles.title: MTR Raises $2.4 Billion From First Public Hong Kong Dollar Bonds
articles.content: MTR Corp Ltd. raised HK$18.9 billion ($2.4 billion) in its first-ever Hong Kong dollar public bond sale, as borrowers increasingly rely on the city’s funding market.

articles.title: Hybrid Bond Buybacks Return After War Paused Riskier Debt Deals
articles.content: European companies are once again buying back junior bonds after the market turmoil sparked by the war in the Middle East and the AI-related software selloff put some riskier debt deals on ice.

articles.title: Higher Oil Prices Pose a Dilemma for US Shale
articles.content: Trump wants producers to unleash output, but many remember the ill-fated dash for growth more than a decade ago.

articles.title: Markets Showing Durability in Bounce From March Lows, Strategist Verrone Says
articles.content: “If you’re looking six months out, I still think we’re higher than we are here,” says Christopher Verone, partner and chief market strategist at Strategas, as he examines the factors driving markets higher. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: US-Iran Peace Talk Uncertainty; Warsh Hearing; Apple Names New CEO | Bloomberg Brief 4/21/2026
articles.content: US equity futures rise as traders await to see whether Iran will participate in further peace negotiations with the US. President Trump's Fed Chair nominee Kevin Warsh will appear before the Senate Banking Committee for a confirmation hearing. Apple appoints John Ternus as CEO to replace Tim Cook in September. Ukraine Deputy Prime Minister Taras Kacha says Ukraine is open to not receive the EU Common Agricultural Policy funding initially. Kevin Mahn of Hennion and Walsh Asset Management says investors have looked past the war with Iran and are focused on earnings. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Stock Market’s Rally to Record High Masks Weak Building Blocks
articles.content: The record-breaking advance in US stocks has many of the hallmarks of an unloved rally, including narrow leadership, low volumes and muted investor sentiment.

articles.title: Equifax Posts Fastest Revenue Growth Since 2021 on Mortgages
articles.content: Equifax Inc. reported its fastest revenue growth in more than four years, driven by a surge in mortgage applications.

articles.title: Magnum Ice Cream Ranks Among Biggest Short Seller Bets in Europe
articles.content: Magnum Ice Cream Co., the maker of Ben & Jerry’s, Breyers and Klondike bars, has become one of the most popular short bets in Europe as it grapples with shrinking profits and changing consumer appetites.

articles.title: Why America is falling out of love with Israel

articles.content: Company code-named Project Prometheus is working on models for industrial applications

articles.title: Switch Lands $2.6 Billion of Bank Pledges to Power Data Centers
articles.content: Switch Inc. gathered $2.6 billion of bank pledges to help the data-center operator procure electricity, a mounting challenge for the booming industry and one of its biggest costs.

articles.title: Fed Chair Nominee Kevin Warsh to Testify at Confirmation Hearing

articles.title: Flurry of US IPOs Race to Tap Market Ahead of SpaceX Debut
articles.content: The US IPO market has gone from desolate to bustling in a matter of weeks, with companies looking to raise as much as $17.3 billion this month alone and capitalize on stocks’ resilience.

articles.title: SEC Monitoring ‘Emerging Pressures’ in Private Credit Space
articles.content: The Securities and Exchange Commission is closely monitoring the “emerging pressures” in the private credit market as redemption requests persist amidst rising default-rate projections, Chairman Paul Atkins said in prepared remarks.

articles.title: Germany's Merz Slams Hostile Bank Tactics; Exclusive UniCredit CEO Interview | The Pulse 4/21
articles.content:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests:
Andrea Orcel, UniCredit, CEO; Anna Rosenberg, Amundi Investment Institute, Head of Geopolitics; Dambisa Moyo, Versaca Investments, Principal; Jimmy Muchechetere, Investec, Senior Equity Analyst. 
 (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Lira Swaps Signal Turkey Will Hold Off Hiking Rates This Meeting
articles.content: Swaps tied to the Turkish lira are signaling that traders have pared bets for the central bank to raise interest rates, after the ceasefire in the Iran war pushed oil prices off their highs and eased inflation fears.

articles.title: Vanke Wins Backing for Bond Delay Plan, Easing Default Risk
articles.content: China Vanke Co. won enough creditor backing to extend payments on a yuan bond due Thursday for a year, easing the embattled developer’s risk of an imminent default, at least for now.

articles.title: Baird Capital Raises $450 Million for Third Private Equity Fund
articles.content: The private equity arm of financial services firm Robert W. Baird & Co. has raised $450 million for a fund that will invest in companies in the US and the UK.

articles.title: Meloni Demands Terna CEO Choose: €7.3 Million Payout or Eni Role
articles.content: Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni said the outgoing chief executive officer of grid operator Terna SpA, Giuseppina di Foggia, should choose between a €7.3 million ($8.6 million) severance or a new role as chairwoman of ENI SpA, making clear the government’s stance that she can’t have both.

articles.title: Inflación y nivel de vida en Sudamérica: qué país ofrece mejor poder adquisitivo y en qué lugar quedó Argentina
articles.content: Un informe privado comparó salarios, precios, vivienda, alimentos y servicios en seis países de la región. Chile lidera en ingresos promedio, Uruguay aparece como el más caro y Argentina combina salarios altos con costos elevados en varios rubros.

articles.title: US Stocks Muted as Traders Look Ahead to Warsh, Iran Updates

articles.title: Magnum Ice Cream Among Biggest Short Seller Bets in Europe
articles.content: Magnum Ice Cream Co., the maker of Ben & Jerry’s, Breyers and Klondike bars, has become one of the most popular short bets in Europe as it grapples with shrinking profits and changing consumer appetites.

articles.title: German Pension VBL Shifts €7 Billion Housing Portfolio Into Fund
articles.content: German public-sector pension fund Versorgungsanstalt des Bundes und der Länder, or VBL, is transferring its residential property portfolio worth around €7 billion ($8.2 billion) into a new fund structure.

articles.title: Billionaire Fredriksen’s Firm Faces $1 Billion Claim After Failed Fraud Trial
articles.content: Two former executives at billionaire John Fredriksen’s oil trading firm sued for about $1 billion in over allegations that the shipping tycoon’s failed “vindictive” lawsuit brought them to the “brink of financial ruin.”

articles.title: Stocks Rise on Strong Earnings as Iran in Focus: Markets Wrap
articles.content: A slew of strong corporate earnings lifted stocks, which also rose as oil dropped on hopes Iran will join talks with the US to end a war that has rattled global markets, jeopardizing the outlook for global economic growth.

articles.title: Romania’s Premier Faces Ultimatum to Quit in Escalating Crisis
articles.content: The biggest party in Romania’s ruling coalition warned it will withdraw ministers from the government, raising pressure on Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan to step down and pushing the four-way alliance further to the brink of collapse.

articles.title: Polish Arms Maker Set for Record Sales on Defense Spending Spree
articles.content: Poland’s biggest defense group is targeting another record year as the European Union’s largest land force ramps up spending to counter threats from Russia and tap into new EU funding.

articles.title: Odd Lots: QXO’s Brad Jacobs on His Big Insulation Bet (Podcast)
articles.content: He’s done it again. On Sunday night, building supply company QXO announced that it would be acquiring TopBuild for $17 billion. TopBuild sells and installs insulation for both the residential and commercial markets. For Brad Jacobs, the CEO of QXO, this is just the latest in a lifetime of deals he’s made. In fact, he’s made over 500 deals in his life across numerous public companies that he’s founded, most of which have XO somewhere in the ticker. Brad’s companies all tend to be highly focused o

articles.title: Rising Costs Threaten the Airline Boom
articles.content: Airlines are walking a tightrope: strong demand and pricing power on one side, rising fuel and labor costs on the other. TD Cowen analyst Tom Fitzgerald joined Bloomberg Open Interest to break down why baggage fees are sticking, why government bailouts are unlikely, and how long airlines can keep passing costs to consumers before demand starts cracking. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.content: US natural gas futures increased as traders monitored potential talks between the US and Iran, pushing up the price of oil and European natural gas that triggered financial inflows into baskets of energy products including US gas. The US gas contract erased earlier losses caused by mild weather and above-average stockpiles.

articles.content: Treasuries fell as strong economic data and higher oil prices prompted traders to scale back rate-cut expectations, overshadowing testimony from Federal Reserve Chair nominee Kevin Warsh.

articles.title: US Says It Boarded Oil Tanker, First During Iranian Blockade
articles.content: The US government said it stopped and boarded a sanctioned oil tanker in its campaign to disrupt Iran’s shipping network, the first such intervention since the imposition of a blockade just over a week ago.

articles.title: Mercuria Was Able to Get Ships Out Through Hormuz, CEO Says
articles.content: Mercuria Energy Group Ltd. was reasonably well positioned before the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, and has been able to get ships out even after war erupted at the end of February, its co-founder and chief executive officer said.

articles.title: Oil Rises as NYT Reports US-Iran Talks on Hold as Deadline Looms
articles.content: Oil prices rose on a New York Times report that US Vice President JD Vance’s planned trip to Islamabad to discuss an end to the Iran war has been put on hold after Tehran failed to respond to US negotiating positions.

articles.title: Sen. Tillis Calls for End to Powell Probe During Warsh Hearing
articles.content:
Republican Sen. Thom Tillis called on the Department of Justice to end its probe into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell for the Fed building cost overruns so he can support Kevin Warsh’s nomination.
Tillis praised Warsh during the Senate hearing for Trump’s pick for Fed chair, saying, “I look forward to supporting your nomination, and I look forward to this investigation being taken down.” Tillis expanded on the Fed building's renovation costs, saying, “There were a variety of reasons why this building went over budget. And as a matter of fact, if we put everybody in prison in federal government that had had a budget go over, we’d have to reserve an area roughly the size of Texas for a penal colony because of way government projects work.” (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Stocks Extend Drop on Report Vance’s Trip on Hold: Markets Wrap

articles.title: Tim Cook's Exit, Warsh Faces Congress | Open Interest 4/21/2026
articles.content: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Matt Miller and Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." Apple’s era shifts as Tim Cook steps down after 15 years, with John Ternus set to take over.  And Kevin Warsh faces Capitol Hill in a high-stakes Fed showdown. We'll take you to Capitol Hill. Plus, UnitedHealth rebounds with higher premiums despite shrinking enrollment, and airlines juggle strong demand against rising costs—how long can travelers keep footing the bill. Bloomberg's Ike Swetlitz and Tom Fitzgerald of TD Cowen break it all down. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: US Natural Gas Extends Gains on Global Oil, Gas Price Increase
articles.content: US natural gas futures ended higher for a fifth consecutive session as traders monitored potential talks between the US and Iran, pushing up the price of oil and European natural gas and triggering financial inflows into baskets of energy products including US gas. The US gas contract erased earlier losses caused by mild weather and above-average stockpiles.

articles.title: Terna CEO Ready to Waive Severance After Italy’s Meloni Demand
articles.content: The outgoing chief executive officer of grid operator Terna SpA, Giuseppina di Foggia, is willing to waive a €7.3 million ($8.6 million) severance to take a new role as chairwoman of ENI SpA after Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni said she had to decide between the two options.

articles.title: Stocks Decline as US-Iran Talks Remain Uncertain: Markets Wrap

articles.title: Vance Trip to Pakistan for Talks With Iran Is On Hold, NYT Reports
articles.content: The New York Times reports that Vice President JD Vance's trip to Pakistan for peace talks with Iran is on hold for now. We hear more from Republican strategist Lanhee Chen and Jeanne Sheehan Zaino, a Bloomberg contributor, on "Balance of Power." (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Warsh's Undisclosed Investments Raise Questions on Influence Says Richards
articles.content: Former Treasury official Kitty Richards, senior strategic adviser at the Groundwork Collaborative, said the financial disclosures of Kevin Warsh, the nominee to be chair of the Federal Reserve, leave some important questions unanswered because some of his holdings remain under wraps. Richards also spoke about questions around Fed independence, saying that Warsh's stances indicate he is more interested in the 'way the political winds are blowing' than the data.  (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.content: An index for emerging-market dropped, reversing earlier gains, amid uncertainty over whether Vice President JD Vance will travel to Islamabad for peace talks as the clock ticks down to the ceasefire deadline on Wednesday.

articles.title: Senator Tillis Floats Off-Ramp to Move Warsh’s Fed Nomination Forward
articles.content: Senator Thom Tillis, a Republican from North Carolina, says that he would be in favor of a joint committee investigation into the oversight of renovations at the Federal Reserve. Tyler Kendall reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: EEUU: ventas minoristas subieron un 1,7% en marzo, mejor de lo previsto
articles.content: Es en comparación con febrero, cuya cifra se revisó al alza. La cifra de ventas minoristas subyacentes -excluyendo materiales de construcción, vehículos y piezas de vehículos, gasolineras y servicios de alimentación- subió un 0,7% en términos intermensuales, frente al aumento del 0,2% que esperaban los analistas.

articles.title: Oil Rises With Doubts Over US-Iran Talks as Truce Winds Down
articles.content: Oil prices rose as doubts festered over whether the US and Iran will hold fresh talks to secure a peace deal before a ceasefire expires on Wednesday, with both sides still at odds on several key issues.

articles.title: Deutsche Telekom Weighs Full Combination With T-Mobile
articles.content: Deutsche Telekom AG is considering a full combination with its American arm T-Mobile US Inc., a move that would create a multinational telecom group and rank as the largest-ever public M&A deal, people with knowledge of the matter said.

articles.title: Warsh Says Fed ‘Must Stay in Its Lane’
articles.content: Bloomberg's Mike McKee detailed changes to the Federal Reserve that fed chair nominee Kevin Warsh revealed in his confirmation hearing Tuesday, saying that Warsh may want to change or eliminate economic guidance. Warsh told Senators that he is not a fan of the Fed's forward guidance and feels that it sticks to it too closely. Warsh also indicated that he would aim to hem in federal reserve members speaking out ahead of rate decisions.  (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Top Oil Traders Say Billion-Barrel Shock to Echo Long After War
articles.content: The impact of the Iran war will continue for months even after any deal to restore shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, the world’s largest oil traders have warned. Some said flows through the waterway may never return to normal.

articles.title: Oil Traders Say Billion-Barrel Hole Will Linger Long After War
articles.content: The impact of the Iran war will continue for months even after any deal to restore shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, the world’s largest oil traders have warned. Some said flows through the waterway may never return to normal. Speaking at the FT Commodities Global Summit in Lausanne, executives at some of the world’s largest oil traders warned that the rewiring of the oil market would take months even if a peace deal is agreed soon. The market is not fully reflecting the impact of the massive supply disruption, they said, cautioning that prices will need to ratchet higher to the point of pushing the global economy toward a recession if the conflict continues. Samantha Dart, Goldman Sachs Co-Head of Global Commodities Research, joins Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss. She speaks with Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Investor-Friendly Bill Seeks to Revive Bolivia’s Gas Sector

articles.content: Los analistas del mercado celebraron las declaraciones sobre la autonomía del banco central de EEUU, aunque reconocieron la falta de detalles en sus declaraciones.

articles.title: Las expectativas de los industriales no mejoran: menos del 4% prevé aumentar el empleo de acá a mitad de año
articles.content: De acuerdo con una encuesta publicada por el INDEC, la demanda insuficiente y la competencia de productos importados siguen siendo los principales problemas del sector.

articles.title: BHP Concludes Months of Talks With China’s Iron Ore Buyer
articles.content: BHP Group said it has finalized a supply agreement with China’s state-backed iron ore buyer, ending a tense standoff that had led to product restrictions and roiled the market.

articles.title: Trump Extends Iran Truce as Talks Falter
articles.content: President Donald Trump has announced he is extending a ceasefire with Iran indefinitely a day before it was set to expire, even as plans for a fresh round of talks between the two countries fall apart. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Ambassador James F. Jeffrey on Trump extends Iran truce, blockade
articles.content: President Donald Trump announced he was extending a ceasefire with Iran indefinitely a day before it was set to expire, even as plans for a fresh round of talks between the two countries fell apart. In a Truth Social post, Trump said Tuesday he would maintain a blockade over ships coming to and from Iran in the Strait of Hormuz. He said Pakistan, which had mediated between the two sides, asked for the US to hold off on fresh strikes and he was extending the ceasefire until Iran submits a new proposal “and discussions are concluded, one way or the other.” Ambassador James F. Jeffrey, Philip Solondz Distinguished Fellow at The Washington Institute and former Deputy National Security Advisor to President George W. Bush, joins Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss. He speaks with Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Goldman Says US Buyers Return to Japan Stocks as War Shock Fades
articles.content: US investors are returning to Japanese stocks as confidence recovers from the initial shock of the Middle East crisis, according to Goldman Sachs Japan Co.’s chief Japan equity strategist.

articles.title: Memory Stock Valuations Spark Debate Over ‘Supercycle’ Potential
articles.content: Memory makers are riding surging demand to record profits, yet their stocks are still trading at a fraction of the valuation multiples of other top artificial intelligence chip names.

articles.title: Trump’s Showdown With Tillis Complicates Warsh Path to Fed Chair
articles.content: The biggest question facing Kevin Warsh was answered before he even reached the committee room Tuesday when President Donald Trump doubled down on a criminal probe that is effectively blocking his own nominee from taking over at the Federal Reserve.

articles.title: SpaceX strikes $60bn deal to acquire AI start-up Cursor
articles.content: Elon Musk’s rocket and AI conglomerate is seeking to catch up to rivals OpenAI and Anthropic

articles.title: Salarios: tras la caída de febrero, se agranda la brecha entre sectores y persisten las dudas sobre una recuperación
articles.content: El salario promedio del Sistema Integrado Previsional Argentino (SIPA) mostró una baja en febrero, aunque en la gestión de Milei superan levemente a la inflación. Sin embargo, hacia el interior del indicador, los haberes de convenio -que alcanzan a millones de trabajadores- acumulan una baja real de 7 puntos porcentuales desde noviembre de 2023.

articles.title: US Stock Futures Rise, Dollar Falls on Iran Truce: Markets Wrap
articles.content: US equity-index futures climbed and the dollar edged lower after President Donald Trump said he would extend the ceasefire with Iran, spurring cautious optimism among investors.

articles.title: Can the Iran Conflict Become a Forever War?
articles.content: Jessica Genauer, UNSW Public Policy Institute Academic Director, says the US and Iran remain far apart on all deeper policy issues, while neither side sees benefit in the resumption of armed conflict. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Cochlear Plunges Most in 30 Years After Profit Guidance Cut
articles.content: Cochlear Ltd. shares plummeted the most in more than 30 years after the Australian company cut its fiscal year profit guidance.

articles.title: Treasury Wine Jumps Most in 12 Years on Strong China Results
articles.content: Shares in Treasury Wine Estates Ltd. rose the most in 12 years after the Australian vintner announced a sharp jump in sales to retailers during the first months of 2026.

articles.title: Crypto Whale Li Moves 20-Strong Trading Team to Tap Asia’s Rich
articles.content: Crypto tycoon Li Lin is moving an entire trading team of about 20 people from his single-family office to Bitfire Group Holdings Ltd., a wealth manager in which he owns a 30% stake, to help the firm tap more private banking clients.

articles.title: Asia’s Largest Oil Buyers Running Low on Hormuz Alternatives
articles.content: Asia’s largest oil buyers have been able to lean on workarounds to limit the impact of more than seven weeks of war in the Persian Gulf, shielding not only their own economies but those of neighbors competing for cargoes.

articles.title: Anthropic’s Mythos Accessed by Unauthorized Users
articles.content: A small group of unauthorized users have accessed Anthropic PBC's new Mythos AI model, which the company says can enable dangerous cyberattacks. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: VW to export more China-made EVs in global sales drive
articles.content: Strategy pivot coincides with slowdown and fierce competition in world’s largest automotive market

articles.title: BHP Strikes Iron Ore Deal With China After Months of Talks
articles.content: BHP Group has struck a supply deal with China’s state-backed iron ore buyer, ending a months-long standoff between a vital consumer and the world’s largest miner that has roiled markets and hampered steel mills’ access to key products.

articles.title: Middle East Conflict Weighs on Thai Banks as Profits Slip
articles.content: Top Thai banks struck a cautious tone on their 2026 outlook after reporting weak first-quarter net income, underscoring growing risks from Middle East conflict and a global energy shock.

articles.title: China Exporters Beset by Yuan Surge Look to Sell Dollar Rallies
articles.content: Gloria Yu’s company exports bicycles and related accessories to as far as Europe and Africa. Having suffered “heavy losses” on some orders due to the yuan’s rapid gains earlier in the year, Yu is looking to better manage her dollar exposure.

articles.title: Warsh rejects accusations he would be Trump’s ‘sock puppet’
articles.content: Senators grill nominee to head Federal Reserve over president’s demands for interest rate cuts

articles.title: SpaceX obtains right to buy AI start-up Cursor for $60bn
articles.content: Elon Musk’s rocket and AI conglomerate is seeking to catch up to rivals OpenAI and Anthropic
